---
title: "Each patient data collection trajectory"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE) 
```

```{r}
# =============================================================================
# Data S6 — per-patient timecourse of daily calories, diet Faith diversity,
# and fecal alpha-diversity. Produces a single multi-page PDF: an enlarged
# example patient sits top-left on page 1, every other patient is a small
# panel, and each page carries one shared "Transplant day" x-axis label.
#
# To run: set the paths in the CONFIG block, then source() this file.
# =============================================================================
library(ggpubr)
library(tidyverse)
library(cowplot)

# ----- CONFIG ----------------------------------------------------------------
# Everything that might need changing lives here, so the rest is untouched.

cfg <- list(
  # input files
  dtb_file        = '../data/152_combined_DTB.csv',
  meta_file       = '../data/153_combined_META.csv',
  faith_file      = '../data/005_faith_pd/alpha-diversity.tsv',
  engraft_file    = '../data/085_engraftment_day_annot.csv',

  # output
  out_file        = '../data/dataS6_085_all_patients_timecourse.pdf',

  # the example patient shown enlarged, with the detailed Diet:/Stool: labels
  example_pid     = 'P1',

  # plotting constants
  scale_factor    = 92,          # 4600/50 — brings stool diversity onto the calorie axis
  y_limit         = 4600,        # shared upper bound so every panel is comparable
  pt_line_size    = 0.2,
  axis_line_thick = 0.2,
  diet_color      = '#E41A1C',
  stool_color     = 'blue',
  faith_color     = 'black',

  panels_per_page = 40,          # 5 columns x 8 rows on an A4 sheet
  ncol            = 5
)
```


```{r}
# ----- LOAD & RESHAPE DATA ---------------------------------------------------
# Each dataset is split into a per-patient list so a panel can be built by
# simply looking up one patient's name.

dtb  <- read_csv(cfg$dtb_file)
meta <- read_csv(cfg$meta_file) |> split(~ pid)

# daily calories: sum every food record within a patient-day
day_calori <- dtb |>
  group_by(pid, fdrt) |>
  summarise(daycal = sum(Calories_kcal), .groups = 'drop') |>
  split(~ pid)

# diet Faith diversity: the sample id packs patient and day as "<pid>d<day>"
faith <- read_tsv(cfg$faith_file) |>
  rename(sampleid = 1) |>                       # first column comes in unnamed
  separate(sampleid, into = c('pid', 'fdrt'), sep = 'd') |>
  mutate(fdrt = as.numeric(fdrt)) |>             # separate() returns text; the axis needs a number
  split(~ pid)

# patients carried through the figure are those that actually have stool data
pids <- names(meta)

# per-patient annotation: engraftment day, diet-day count, stool-sample count
engraftment <- read_csv(cfg$engraft_file) |>
  filter(pid %in% pids)

diet_days <- dtb |>
  filter(pid %in% pids) |>
  distinct(pid, fdrt) |>
  count(pid, name = 'd_days')

stool_days <- read_csv(cfg$meta_file) |>
  filter(pid %in% pids) |>
  distinct(pid, sdrt) |>
  count(pid, name = 's_days')

annot <- engraftment |>
  full_join(diet_days, by = 'pid') |>
  full_join(stool_days, by = 'pid') |>
  split(~ pid)

# a quick look at the value ranges, handy when sanity-checking y_limit
message('Faith pd range:  ', paste(range(read_csv(cfg$faith_file)$faith_pd), collapse = ' - '))
message('Daily cal range: ', paste(range(day_calori |> bind_rows() |> pull(daycal)), collapse = ' - '))

# ----- PANEL BUILDER ---------------------------------------------------------
# One function builds any patient's panel. 'detailed = TRUE' gives the enlarged
# example look (axis titles, "Diet:"/"Stool:" prefixes); FALSE gives the bare
# small-panel look used for the grid.

make_panel <- function(pid, detailed = FALSE) {

  # the count labels differ only in whether they carry a "Diet:"/"Stool:" prefix
  diet_label  <- if (detailed) 'Diet: {d_days} days'    else '{d_days} days'
  stool_label <- if (detailed) 'Stool: {s_days} samples' else '{s_days} samples'

  p <- ggplot() +
    # daily caloric intake
    geom_line(data = day_calori[[pid]], aes(fdrt, daycal),
              linewidth = cfg$pt_line_size, colour = cfg$diet_color) +
    # diet Faith diversity
    geom_line(data = faith[[pid]], aes(fdrt, faith_pd),
              linewidth = cfg$pt_line_size, colour = cfg$faith_color) +
    # fecal diversity, scaled up onto the calorie axis
    geom_line(data = meta[[pid]], aes(sdrt, simpson_reciprocal * cfg$scale_factor),
              linewidth = cfg$pt_line_size, colour = cfg$stool_color) +
    # engraftment day, and transplant day zero
    geom_vline(data = annot[[pid]], aes(xintercept = engraftment_day),
               colour = 'forestgreen', linewidth = cfg$axis_line_thick, linetype = 'dashed') +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = 'darkgray', linewidth = cfg$axis_line_thick) +
    # the two count annotations, pinned to the top-right corner
    geom_text(data = annot[[pid]], inherit.aes = FALSE,
              aes(Inf, Inf, label = str_glue(diet_label)),
              hjust = 1.6, vjust = 1, size = 2, colour = cfg$diet_color) +
    geom_text(data = annot[[pid]], inherit.aes = FALSE,
              aes(Inf, Inf, label = str_glue(stool_label)),
              hjust = 1.3, vjust = 2.5, size = 2, colour = cfg$stool_color) +
    theme_pubr()

  if (detailed) {
    # the enlarged example carries full axis titles on both sides
    p +
      scale_y_continuous(
        name = 'Daily calories, Faith div', limits = c(0, cfg$y_limit),
        sec.axis = sec_axis(~ . / cfg$scale_factor,
                            name = expression(Fecal ~ alpha ~ diversity))) +
      labs(x = 'Transplant day', y = '', title = pid) +
      theme(axis.text       = element_text(size = 5),
            axis.title      = element_text(size = 8),
            plot.title      = element_text(size = 8),
            axis.title.y    = element_text(colour = cfg$diet_color),
            axis.title.y.right = element_text(colour = cfg$stool_color),
            axis.line       = element_line(colour = 'black', linewidth = cfg$axis_line_thick),
            axis.ticks      = element_line(colour = 'black', linewidth = cfg$axis_line_thick),
            aspect.ratio    = 1/2)
  } else {
    # the small grid panels drop axis titles and the left y numbers to cut clutter;
    # tick marks stay so each panel keeps its own scale
    p +
      scale_y_continuous(name = '', limits = c(0, cfg$y_limit),
                         sec.axis = sec_axis(~ . / cfg$scale_factor)) +
      labs(x = 'Transplant day', y = '', title = pid) +
      theme(axis.text          = element_text(size = 7),
            axis.title         = element_text(size = 5),
            plot.title         = element_text(size = 8),
            axis.title.x       = element_blank(),
            axis.text.y        = element_blank(),
            axis.title.y       = element_text(colour = cfg$diet_color),
            axis.title.y.right = element_text(colour = cfg$stool_color),
            axis.line          = element_line(colour = 'black', linewidth = cfg$axis_line_thick),
            axis.ticks         = element_line(colour = 'black', linewidth = cfg$axis_line_thick),
            aspect.ratio       = 1/2)
  }
}

# build every small panel once, keyed by patient
small_panels <- pids |> set_names() |> map(make_panel, detailed = FALSE)

# the enlarged example patient
example_panel <- make_panel(cfg$example_pid, detailed = TRUE)

# ----- PAGE BUILDER ----------------------------------------------------------
# Assembles one A4 page: bold title on top, panel grid in the middle, and one
# shared "Transplant day" label at the bottom. Page 1 reserves a top-left 2x2
# block for the enlarged example.

xlab_strip <- ggdraw() + draw_label('Transplant day', size = 8)

make_page <- function(panels, page_title, big_panel = NULL) {

  title <- ggdraw() +
    draw_label(page_title, fontface = 'bold', x = 0, hjust = 0) +
    theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 7))

  if (is.null(big_panel)) {
    # ordinary page: a plain 5-column grid
    body <- plot_grid(plotlist = panels, ncol = cfg$ncol)
  } else {
    # page 1: the example fills a top-left 2x2; the first 6 small panels sit
    # to its right, and the remaining 30 fill a normal grid below
    top_right <- plot_grid(plotlist = panels[1:6], ncol = 3)
    top_band  <- plot_grid(big_panel, top_right, ncol = 2, rel_widths = c(2, 3))
    bottom    <- plot_grid(plotlist = panels[7:36], ncol = cfg$ncol)
    body      <- plot_grid(top_band, bottom, ncol = 1, rel_heights = c(2, 5))
  }

  plot_grid(title, body, xlab_strip, ncol = 1, rel_heights = c(0.08, 1, 0.05)) +
    theme(plot.margin = unit(c(1, 1, 2, 1), 'cm'))
}

# ----- SPLIT PATIENTS ACROSS PAGES -------------------------------------------
# Page 1 holds 36 small panels because the example eats 4 cells; later pages
# hold a full 40. The chunking is computed rather than hand-typed so adding or
# losing patients never means re-editing index ranges.
 
n          <- length(pids)
page1_n    <- cfg$panels_per_page - 4                 # 36
later_idx  <- seq(page1_n + 1, n)
later_chunks <- split(later_idx, ceiling(seq_along(later_idx) / cfg$panels_per_page))
 
pages <- c(
  list(make_page(small_panels[1:page1_n], 'Data S6',
                 big_panel = example_panel)),
  map(later_chunks, ~ make_page(small_panels[.x], 'Data S6 (Continued)'))
)
  
# ----- WRITE ONE MULTI-PAGE PDF ----------------------------------------------
# A single pdf() device keeps every print() as its own page, so the four pages
# land in one file with no Illustrator step.
 
pdf(cfg$out_file, width = 210 / 25.4, height = 297 / 25.4)  # A4, mm -> inches
walk(pages, print)
dev.off()
 
message('Wrote ', length(pages), '-page figure to ', cfg$out_file)
```
